file_title: Employee Unionization Trends at Lee's Market
introduction: The purpose of this presentation is to analyze the recent trends in employee unionization at Lee's Market. Our focus will be on evaluating the percentage of employees who were part of a union as of Q2 2026.

subsections.heading: Evolution of Employee Unionization
subsections.content:
• Unionization efforts began in the company in the early 2000s
• By 2010, 30% of employees were part of a union
• In 2015, the company reached a peak of 45% unionization
• Unionization decreased to 35% in 2020 following changes in company policy
• As of 2025, management implemented new strategies to maintain a healthy relationship with unions
• Unions have played a significant role in shaping employee benefits and wage structures
subsections.justification: The slide covers historical evolution of unionization, but doesn't provide information on the percentage of unionized employees in Q2 2026.

subsections.heading: Unionized Employee Percentage - Q2 2026
subsections.content:
• The total number of employees in Q2 2026 was 5,000
• A significant percentage of employees were part of the retail department
• The customer service department had the lowest number of unionized employees
• Unionized employees were primarily full-time rather than part-time
• The average age of unionized employees was 35 years
• The gender distribution among unionized employees was nearly equal
subsections.justification: The slide provides various details about unionized employees in Q2 2026 but doesn't reveal the exact percentage.

subsections.heading: Impact of Unionization on Employee Satisfaction
subsections.content:
• Employee satisfaction surveys indicate a positive correlation with unionization
• Unionized employees reported higher job security
• Non-unionized employees had higher flexibility in job roles
• Overall job satisfaction was not significantly different between unionized and non-unionized employees
• Unionized employees reported increased satisfaction with dispute resolution processes
• Both groups indicated room for improvement in communication with management
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the impact of unionization on employee satisfaction, not the percentage of unionized employees in Q2 2026.

conclusion: Understanding employee unionization trends at Lee's Market is critical for our strategic planning. Further analysis will focus on the implications of these trends for our employee relations strategy.

image_queries.slide_title: Impact of Unionization on Employee Satisfaction
